title: -Request for Proposals for Capacity Building for Emerging Nonprofit Affordable Housing Developers

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing a Request for Proposals (RFP) for $400,000 ($200,000
per year for Fiscal Years 2027 and 2028) to support capacity building for emerging
nonprofit affordable housing developers.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
The RFP process is intended to result in a recommendation to award capacity building
grant funds to one or more qualifying emerging nonprofit affordable housing developers,
up to a total of $200,000 for each fiscal year (FY). The funds would be awarded from
the general fund pursuant to Measure U1 (Fund #016) and would be subject to Council
approval in the FY2027 and FY2028 budget processes.
sections.current_situation:
On November 6, 2025, the Housing Advisory Commission took the following action:
Action: M/S/C (Haycox/Potter) to recommend that City Council release an RFP for
$400,000 ($200,000 per year for FY 2027 and FY 2028) to support capacity building for
emerging nonprofit affordable housing developers; and authorize the City Manager or
designee to execute all original or amended documents or agreements to effectuate
these actions.
Vote: Ayes: Bell, Haycox, Micael, Potter, Scott, Simon-Weisberg, and Twu. Noes: None.
Abstain: None. Absent: Shere.
Housing and Community Services Division staff intend to release an RFP for a total of
$400,000 for two years of capacity building grants to encourage and support new or
less experienced nonprofit developers to build capacity to pursue future affordable
housing opportunities in Berkeley.
Eligible Applicants
The capacity building funds will be available to nonprofit developers with limited
development experience that are actively working on or pursuing new opportunities for

affordable housing in Berkeley that could be eligible for City funding under the Housing
Trust Fund (HTF) program or Small Sites Program (SSP). The City’s HTF and SSP
Guidelines include specific threshold requirements for developer experience for the City
to consider funding predevelopment or development loans; applicants for the capacity
building grants do not need to meet those standards. Applicants can request up to
$200,000 per fiscal year, however if the City receives multiple applications, the available
funds may be allocated to one applicant or divided amongst multiple applicants.
Eligible Uses
Potential uses of the capacity building funds include (but are not limited to) the
following:
 Staff developmen
sections.background:
This RFP will provide an open and transparent process for qualified organizations to
request capacity building support. The selected applicant(s) will enter into the City’s
community agency contract and will be required to report on the use of funds and
progress towards capacity building goals on a quarterly basis.
The City has a history of providing operational support to local nonprofit developers
through Community Housing Development Organization grants to qualified
organizations (currently Resources for Community Development and Satellite
Affordable Housing Associates). The City issued an RFP for capacity building for
developers interested in pursuing SSP projects in 2018, through which Bay Area
Community Land Trust (BACLT) was awarded $50,000. Since then, Council approved
subsequent annual allocations to BACLT in amounts ranging from $100,000 to
$200,000 per fiscal year.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
There are no direct environmental or climate impacts related to issuing the RFP and
awarding capacity building grants.

sections.rationale:
Issuing a new RFP offers a transparent process and allows organizations with limited
development experience to apply. The capacity building funding could help an emerging
developer grow its staffing, improve organizational processes, and be better positioned
to create or preserve affordable housing in Berkeley.
sections.alternatives:
The City could opt to not release the RFP. This could negatively impact emerging
developers’ ability to pursue new affordable housing opportunities in Berkeley.
CONTACT PERSON
Asavari Devadiga, Community Development Project Coordinator, HHCS, 510-981-5426
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 3
Page 4 of 4
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
AUTHORIZING A REQUEST FOR PROPOSALS FOR CAPACITY BUILDING
WHEREAS, the City Council established a Housing Trust Fund (HTF) program to assist
in the development and expansion of housing affordable to low- and moderate-income
persons who either work or reside within the City of Berkeley, and authorized the City
Manager to implement the HTF program; and
WHEREAS, there is a great need for affordable and special needs housing in the City of
Berkeley as stated in the General Plan Housing Element and the City of Berkeley’s
Consolidated Plan; and
WHEREAS, the City intends to release a Request for Proposals (RFP) for capacity
building to support newer or less experienced nonprofit developers to build capacity to
pursue future affordable housing opportunities in Berkeley; and
WHEREAS, up to $200,000 per fiscal year (FY) for FY2027 and FY2028 would be made
available to one or more qualifying developers through the RFP process; and
WHEREAS, the RFP funds are general funds generated pursuant to Measure U1, and
subject to approval by Council in the FY2027 and FY2028 budget processes.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
Council authorizes releasing a Request for Proposals for $400,000 ($200,000 per year
for FY2027 and FY2028) in resources generated by Measure U1 (Fund #016) to support
capacity building for emerging nonprofit affordable housing developers.
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that funding allocations to the selected applicant or
applicants will be subject to Council approval as part of the annual budget approval cycle.
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that the City Manager, or designee, is hereby authorized
to execute all original or amended documents or agreements to effectuate this action.
